steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the function of the underlined sentence within the passage as a whole. The underlined sentence is Cole's team's claim that penguins could fly at some point but lost that ability over 60 million years ago. Look at what comes immediately before and after to determine its role.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — It provides an answer to the question in the previous sentence.: The previous sentence asks "Were penguins always flightless?" The underlined sentence directly answers this by stating they were not always flightless — they lost flight more than 60 million years ago. ✓
  • Choice B — It contradicts a description in the sentence that follows.: The following sentence describes fossil and genetic evidence supporting the researchers' conclusion — it does not contradict the underlined sentence, it supports it. ✗
  • Choice C — It notes that the question in the previous sentence has not been researched.: The opposite is true — Cole's team has researched this question and the underlined sentence presents their finding. ✗
  • Choice D — It defines a scientific term used in the sentence that follows.: No scientific term is defined. The underlined sentence makes a claim about evolutionary history, not a definition. ✗
That matches Choice A — It provides an answer to the question in the previous sentence..
